stories.title: Israel says it killed two top Iranian commanders in targeted strike
stories.description: Israel says it killed Ali Larijani and Gholamreza Soleimani, the highest profile assassinations since the targeting of Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on the first day of the war.

stories.title: Israel's Lebanon incursion raises pressure on West over Iran-backed Hezbollah
stories.description: Western countries fear Israel's ground offensive in Lebanon aims to force them to act on disarming Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah, increasing tensions and urgency for a diplomatic solution.

stories.title: Historian shares how Russia benefits from the US being at war with Iran
stories.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with historian Sergey Radchenko about how Russia benefits from the U.S. being at war with Iran.

stories.title: How Trump's Treasury is shifting sanctions to punish his critics and reward friends
stories.description: Spain's Prime Minister called U.S. strikes against Iran "unjustified." When other foreigners in power have used similar language against the U.S. or Israel, they were sanctioned by the Treasury.

stories.title: How the energy crisis fueled by the war in Iran is affecting Asia
stories.description: Asia is feeling the energy shock of the war on Iran. NPR's A Martinez talks to Sam Reynolds of the Institute for Energy Economics and Financial Analysis.

stories.title: Morning news brief
stories.description: Israel's ground moves in Lebanon raise pressure on Western countries, Europeans leaders reject Trump's demand to help with the Strait of Hormuz, blackout leaves millions without power in Cuba.

stories.title: Millions of migrants in the Middle East caught in the middle of the war in Iran
stories.description: As the war in Iran continues, millions of migrant workers in the Gulf are caught up in the ongoing conflict, adding to their already vulnerable existence.

stories.title: Trump says Europeans should help secure the Strait of Hormuz. So far, they've refused
stories.description: European leaders are rejecting President Trump's demand they send military assets to the Middle East to help open the Strait of Hormuz, which is being obstructed by Iran.

stories.title: How NATO countries are reacting to Trump's request for help with the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: NPR's A Martinez asks Ian Lesser of the German Marshall Fund of the United States how NATO regards President Trump's request for help to secure the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Island-wide blackout knocks out power to millions in Cuba amid ongoing energy crisis
stories.description: Cuba plunges into darkness as nationwide blackouts hits over 10 million people on the island, worsening an energy crisis fueled by oil shortages and U.S. sanctions.

stories.title: Surge in US petrol prices deepens political peril for Trump over Iran
stories.description: The odds of a quick resolution to the war appear to be dwindling by the day, and the political stakes for the president are only growing.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: Israel says 'limited' ground operations under way in Lebanon
stories.description: Troops are targeting "Hezbollah strongholds" in south Lebanon, where strikes reportedly killed nine people on Monday.

stories.title: How might the UK support shipping in the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Ben Chu from BBC Verify has been looking at what role the Royal Navy could play in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Two charged in death of anti-Iranian regime activist in Canada
stories.description: Police say the motive remains under investigation but noted the three had disputes on social media.

stories.title: Who wants what from the Iran war?
stories.description: Most people, although not everybody, want this war to end as quickly as possible. But on what terms?

stories.title: Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
stories.description: Residents describe new security checkpoints around the capital, where they say people are stopped and searched.

stories.title: Iran hits key UAE oil port and Dubai airport
stories.description: The port of Fujairah plays a crucial role in helping keep global supplies moving when the Strait of Hormuz is blocked.

stories.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
stories.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

stories.title: Iranian footballer says 'everything will be fine' as she trains with Australian team
stories.description: Several players were granted Australian visas after concerns the team would face repercussions at home.

stories.title: Iran captain latest footballer to drop asylum bid, Iranian state media says
stories.description: Five of the seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

stories.title: US names six crew killed in refuelling plane crash in Iraq
stories.description: The aircraft was on a combat mission as part of ongoing US operations against Iran when it crashed in western Iraq on Thursday.

stories.title: BBC visits aftermath of Israeli strike on Lebanon that killed family as IDF targets Hezbollah
stories.description: A relative tells BBC those killed were civilians and not Hezbollah operatives, but the Israel Defense Forces says it was targeting "terrorist infrastructure".

stories.title: Why has the US targeted Iran's Kharg Island?
stories.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

stories.title: Hamas urges key ally Iran to halt attacks on Gulf states
stories.description: The Palestinian group also affirms Tehran's right to defend itself from "aggression" by the US and Israel.

stories.title: Gulf states seek to bypass Strait of Hormuz for oil exports via pipelines
stories.description: Iran sends drones to attack the UAE port of Fujairah in the Gulf of Oman, raising the stakes as Gulf states send their blocked crude through pipelines away from the contested Strait of Hormuz. We take a closer look. Also in this edition: central banks face a dilemma as high oil p...

stories.title: Trump's call for help with Hormuz largely falls on deaf ears
stories.description: Donald Trump is finding himself increasingly isolated on the world stage with allies, including NATO countries, are refusing to come to his aid to help to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The EU meanwhile says dipolomatic ways have to found in order to keep the critical waterway open...

stories.title: 'Ready to fight' but lacking means, Iraqi Kurds targeted by Iran
stories.description: Iranian Kurdish opposition groups, united in a coalition since last February, issued a statement this weekend calling on all "oppressed minorities in Iran” to unite in support of a democratic transition. But in Iraqi Kurdistan, these Kurdish parties, in exile for decades, continu...

stories.title: Israel says killed Iran national security chief Larijani
stories.description: Defence Minister Israel Katz said on Tuesday the Israeli military had killed Iran's security chief and the ​head of ‌its Basij militia in airstrikes overnight, and Tehran kept up attacks ⁠against Gulf neighbours that have pushed up energy prices. Katz said in a statement he had b...

stories.title: Israel hits Lebanon with fresh strikes as ground operation expands
stories.description: The Israeli military launched a fresh wave of air strikes early Tuesday simultaneously targeting Iran and Lebanon, with Beirut and the southern Lebanese city of Tyre among the areas hit. Israel is also expanding a ground operation in the south of Lebanon. France 24’s Claire Pacca...

stories.title: US embassy in Baghdad attacked as Iraq pulled deeper into Mideast war
stories.description: A drone and rocket attack targeted the US embassy in Baghdad early Tuesday, while a strike killed four people at a house reportedly hosting Iranian advisors, security officials said, pulling Iraq deeper into the Middle East war.

stories.title: Why is Trump looking to China to help open the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: US president Donald Trump has called on numerous nations, including China, to help ships safely ‌transit the key Strait of Hormuz, where one-fifth of the world's oil transits on a daily basis. But why has Trump turned to Beijing, so often an adversary of the US and ally of Iran, ...

stories.title: 'Like buying a ticket to the Titanic': EU leaders reject Trump's Hormuz demands
stories.description: EU leaders have almost universally rejected US president Donald Trump’s demand to send warships to escort oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz as the continent’s leaders strive to stay out of the war in the Middle East, with one French official going as far to say that partic...

stories.title: Ghassan Salamé urges Israel, international community to let Lebanon 'do its job more efficiently'
stories.description: Amid Israel's further military escalation and Hezbollah's "illegal armed activities", Lebanon is confronted with an acute humanitarian and political crisis. To better understand the impact the conflict is having all across Lebanese society, FRANCE 24's Mark Owen is pleased to wel...

stories.title: Iran 'negotiating' with FIFA over moving World Cup games from US to Mexico
stories.description: Iran's football federation is holding discussions with global governing body FIFA about moving the Islamic Republic's World Cup matches from the United States to Mexico. US President Donald Trump said last week that Iran's team should not travel to the tournament "for their own l...

stories.title: Middle East war live: Israel killed Iran’s security chief Ali Larijani, Defence Minister Katz says
stories.description: Israel has killed Iran's powerful national security chief Ali Larijani in an overnight strike, Defence Minister Israel Katz said Tuesday. Gholamreza Soleimani, the head of Iran's volunteer Basij paramilitary force, was reportedly also killed in a strike. Iran has not yet commente...

stories.title: Social media abuzz with viral rumours of Netanyahu's death
stories.description: Social media has recently been abuzz with false rumours claiming that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was killed in an Iranian missile strike. Vedika Bahl explores the viral claims around Netanyahu's death and the continuing online speculation in this edition of Truth o...

stories.title: Oil eases amid signs Iran is allowing some tankers to pass through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Global oil prices fell sharply on Monday after a Pakistan-bound oil tanker passed through the Strait of Hormuz with its transponder on. The decline came even as countries have so far rebuffed Donald Trump's request to send warships to escort vessels through the waterway. Meanwhil...

stories.title: Could the Iran war trigger a global recession?
stories.description: Energy prices are surging as the Iran war disrupts supply, raising risks for the US, China and Europe.

stories.title: Israel says Iran’s security chief Larijani has been killed
stories.description: Israel’s Defence Minister Israel Katz says Ali Larijani, Iran’s security chief, has been killed in an Israeli strike.

stories.title: Israel says it’s killed Iran’s security chief Larijani and Basij commander
stories.description: Israeli Defence Minister Katz has claimed that Iran’s top security official has been killed; Iran is yet to comment.

stories.title: Gulf economies suffer brunt of Iran war as recession risk looms
stories.description: From energy exports to tourism, the economies of Gulf nations are at risk from a prolonged conflict in the Middle East.

stories.title: Why the Iranian regime did not collapse after Khamenei’s assassination
stories.description: The Iranian regime is designed to survive power vacuums. That, however, does not mean it will survive the war unscathed.

stories.title: Israel claims to have assassinated commander of Iran’s Basij militia unit
stories.description: If confirmed, Soleimani would be the highest-level assassination in war since US-Israeli forces killed Ali Khamenei.

stories.title: Iran is targeting ships in response to US attacks — can the US stop them?
stories.description: As the US-Israeli war against Iran continues, we look at a scenario reminiscent of the tanker war of the 1980s

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 18 of US-Israel attacks?
stories.description: The US-Israeli attacks target Iranian cities as the conflict spreads to the region and causes a global energy crisis.

stories.title: Drone sparks fire at UAE oil site as Gulf takes more hits amid Iran war
stories.description: The Fujairah oil hub has been hit repeatedly by Iranian drones as the US-Israeli war continues to ignite instability.

stories.title: Inside Qeshm, Iran’s underground missile fortress and geological marvel
stories.description: From the Valley of Stars to underground missiles, Qeshm Island is strategic ground zero for the US-Israel war on Iran.

stories.title: Iran’s neighbours brace for fallout as war threatens new refugee crisis
stories.description: As war engulfs Iran, neighbouring states quietly prepare for a potential refugee exodus across already fragile borders.

stories.title: Iran negotiating with FIFA to move World Cup 2026 matches from US to Mexico
stories.description: The national football federation wants to relocate its group matches to Mexico due to the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran.

stories.title: EU says Iran is ‘not Europe’s war’
stories.description: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said there is no appetite to expand Europe’s naval mission to the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Marine turned anti-war protester says Trump wrong on Israel, Iran
stories.description: Former Marine Brian McGinnis says Donald Trump is “wrong” on Israel and Iran.

stories.title: Iran delivers new strikes against enemy targets — news agency
stories.description: As of now, there is no information on which US or Israeli facilities were attacked

stories.title: No disruption in Iran’s oil exports after US strikes on Kharg Island — Iranian parliament
stories.description: Enemy ships belonging to the United States, Israel, and their allies are not allowed to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, Esmail Hosseini said

stories.title: Military escort in Strait of Hormuz to reanimate navigation by only 10% — newspaper
stories.description: Earlier, US President Donald Trump said that countries importing oil through the Strait of Hormuz must help ensure the safety of maritime navigation by sending military vessels there

stories.title: Martial law in Strait of Hormuz prevents US from supplying its bases — Iranian ambassador
stories.description: According to Kazem Jalali, Iran is taking measures to disrupt the enemy’s logistics for its own security

stories.title: Letter by top Iranian security official claimed dead by Israel emerges on social media
stories.description: This is the condolence message from Ali Larijani over the death of naval servicemen killed in a US strike on the IRIS Dena frigate off the coast of Sri Lanka

stories.title: Regional war with Iran will not diminish importance of global conflict in Ukraine — expert
stories.description: The Iran-related tensions are primarily regional, lacking the extensive frontlines and trench warfare characterizing Ukraine’s war, Abdullah Agar said

stories.title: Press review: Tehran pursues attrition as Trump delays China visit amid Iran tensions
stories.description: Top stories from the Russian press on Tuesday, March 17th

stories.title: Trump wanted Iranian military to bow to him, but this scenario failed — ambassador
stories.description: Kazem Jalali stressed that Iran’s political system proved far more resilient
